message:
frankentorch-4ak: add fake-quantize MVP and MinMaxObserver

ft-api (FrankenTorchSession):
- tensor_quantize_per_tensor(input, scale, zero_point, qmin, qmax)
  -> clamp(round(x/scale) + zero_point, qmin, qmax). Output values are
  integers in [qmin, qmax] but stored in the input dtype (fake quant).
- tensor_dequantize_per_tensor(quantized, scale, zero_point)
  -> (q - zero_point) * scale.
- tensor_fake_quantize_per_tensor: dequantize ∘ quantize. Differentiable
  via tensor_round's straight-through estimator, suitable for QAT.
- Validates qparams: scale finite & positive, qmin < qmax,
  zero_point ∈ [qmin, qmax].

ft-nn:
- QParams { scale, zero_point, qmin, qmax } with qint8/quint8 helpers.
- MinMaxObserver: running min/max over observed tensors with
  compute_qparams() that derives PyTorch-equivalent affine
  (scale, zero_point), forcing 0 into the represented range.
  Skips non-finite values.

Tests (ft-nn):
- quantize/dequantize round-trip recovery within scale.
- out-of-range inputs clamp to qmin/qmax.
- invalid qparams rejected (zero/negative/NaN scale, qmin>=qmax,
  zero_point outside range).
- fake_quantize idempotence + within-scale recovery.
- observer tracks min/max, skips non-finite, computes correct qparams.
- end-to-end calibration: observer -> qparams -> fake_quantize.

Deferred work tracked in frankentorch-hpg: QInt8/QUInt8 storage
dtypes, QuantizedLinear with packed INT8 weights, per-channel
quantization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankentorch-40i","title":"Fix f32 index_select/gather/scatter output dtype","description":"Audit: index_select/gather/scatter in ft-autograd always emit F64 tensors even when input dtype is F32, despite f32 CPU kernels existing. This silently upcasts and breaks dtype preservation. Fix by dispatching to f32 kernels and emitting F32 storage, add f32 forward tests in ft-api.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:18:13.533424703Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:25:26.015235117Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:25:26.014918859Z","close_reason":"Implemented f32 index_select/gather/scatter dispatch + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-4ak","title":"Add basic quantization support (torch.quantization equivalent)","description":"PyTorch's quantization allows running models with INT8 weights for faster inference and smaller model size. This is increasingly important for edge deployment.

Implementation plan (CPU quantization only):
1. Add quantized dtypes to ft-core: QInt8, QUInt8 (with scale and zero_point metadata)
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta extending TensorMeta with quantization parameters
3. Add quantize/dequantize functions:
   - session.quantize_per_tensor(input, scale, zero_point, dtype) -> quantized tensor
   - session.dequantize(quantized) -> float tensor
4. Add quantized linear layer (QuantizedLinear) in ft-nn:
   - Stores INT8 weights + F32 bias
   - Forward: dequantize weights -> matmul -> add bias (or fused quantized matmul if we add a kernel)
5. Add basic observer (MinMaxObserver) for calibration:
   - Tracks running min/max of tensor values
   - Computes optimal scale/zero_point
6. Add post-training quantization workflow:
   - Calibrate observers on representative data
   - Convert float model to quantized model

Tests:
- Unit test: quantize then dequantize recovers original values (within quantization error)
- Unit test: quantized linear forward matches dequantize-then-matmul
- Unit test: MinMaxObserver correctly tracks min/max
- Unit test: scale/zero_point calculation is correct
- E2E test: train a float model, quantize it, verify inference output is close to float model
- Edge case: quantize with scale=0 should error, not divide by zero","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:38.229580914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:38.229580914Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-4ak","title":"Add basic quantization support (torch.quantization equivalent)","description":"PyTorch's quantization allows running models with INT8 weights for faster inference and smaller model size. This is increasingly important for edge deployment.

Implementation plan (CPU quantization only):
1. Add quantized dtypes to ft-core: QInt8, QUInt8 (with scale and zero_point metadata)
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta extending TensorMeta with quantization parameters
3. Add quantize/dequantize functions:
   - session.quantize_per_tensor(input, scale, zero_point, dtype) -> quantized tensor
   - session.dequantize(quantized) -> float tensor
4. Add quantized linear layer (QuantizedLinear) in ft-nn:
   - Stores INT8 weights + F32 bias
   - Forward: dequantize weights -> matmul -> add bias (or fused quantized matmul if we add a kernel)
5. Add basic observer (MinMaxObserver) for calibration:
   - Tracks running min/max of tensor values
   - Computes optimal scale/zero_point
6. Add post-training quantization workflow:
   - Calibrate observers on representative data
   - Convert float model to quantized model

Tests:
- Unit test: quantize then dequantize recovers original values (within quantization error)
- Unit test: quantized linear forward matches dequantize-then-matmul
- Unit test: MinMaxObserver correctly tracks min/max
- Unit test: scale/zero_point calculation is correct
- E2E test: train a float model, quantize it, verify inference output is close to float model
- Edge case: quantize with scale=0 should error, not divide by zero","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:38.229580914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:51.137107575Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:51.134684781Z","close_reason":"Implemented MVP fake-quantization slice: tensor_quantize_per_tensor / tensor_dequantize_per_tensor / tensor_fake_quantize_per_tensor on FrankenTorchSession (composes round + clamp + scalar shift; STE backward via tensor_round); MinMaxObserver and QParams with qint8/quint8 helpers in ft-nn; calibrated observer -> qparams -> fake_quantize E2E test. Deferred scope (QInt8/QUInt8 storage dtypes, QuantizedLinear, per-channel quantization) tracked in follow-up bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankentorch-hpg","title":"Quantized storage dtypes (QInt8/QUInt8) and QuantizedLinear","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-4ak. The MVP fake-quantization slice landed in 4f6fff6 covers post-training calibration + QAT-style fake quantize using composed round/clamp ops, which keeps storage in the input dtype.

Remaining V1 work to fully match torch.quantization:
1. Add QInt8 / QUInt8 storage dtypes in ft-core (TensorStorage variant + DType enum + serde paths).
2. Add QuantizedTensorMeta carrying scale + zero_point as part of TensorMeta.
3. Add a QuantizedLinear module in ft-nn that stores INT8 weights and dequantizes on the fly (or invokes a fused INT8 matmul kernel when added).
4. Per-channel quantization (per-axis scale/zero_point vectors).
5. Conformance fixtures comparing against torch.quantization output on a couple of canonical layers.

Out of scope here: GPU INT8 kernels (tracked under frankentorch-c3d).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:59.714118325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T02:17:59.714118325Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-i7v","title":"Restore ft-optim rustfmt compliance","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:32:23.707770910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T03:34:06.440692399Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T03:34:06.440430122Z","close_reason":"Completed: restored ft-optim rustfmt compliance","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-iey","title":"Add ONNX model export support","description":"ONNX export allows FrankenTorch models to be deployed in any ONNX-compatible runtime (ONNX Runtime, TensorRT, CoreML, etc.). This is important for production deployment and interoperability.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a new crate ft-onnx (or module in ft-serialize) that:
   a. Traces a model forward pass to capture the computation graph
   b. Maps FrankenTorch ops to ONNX operator set (opset 17+)
   c. Serializes the graph + weights to ONNX protobuf format
2. Add session.export_onnx(model, dummy_input, path) to ft-api
3. Map core ops: Linear, Conv2d, ReLU, BatchNorm, LSTM, Softmax, MatMul, Add, etc.
4. Use the onnx-pb crate for protobuf serialization (or manual protobuf encoding)

Tests:
- Unit test: export a Linear model, verify ONNX file is valid protobuf
- Unit test: exported model contains correct op types and shapes
- Integration test (if onnxruntime-rs available): run exported model and compare outputs
- Edge case: export model with dynamic batch size","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:10.592448782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990921829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankentorch-iey","depends_on_id":"frankentorch-2er","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:19:22.990453482Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankentorch-igu","title":"Add adversarial/fuzz test coverage for high-risk paths (spec Gate G4)","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §5 requires 'adversarial fixtures and fuzz/property suites for high-risk entry points' and §18 defines Gate G4 (adversarial + property tests) as release-blocking.

Current state: ft-serialize has 5 proptest properties. ft-core has none. ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-kernel-cpu have none. The spec's Security Threat Matrix (§16) defines 8 threat categories that need adversarial coverage.

Implementation plan:
1. Add proptest properties for ft-autograd:
   - Random computation graphs maintain gradient correctness (numerical gradient check)
   - Backward on disconnected subgraphs doesn't corrupt other gradients
   - In-place mutation version tracking prevents stale gradient reads
2. Add proptest properties for ft-kernel-cpu:
   - Broadcasting produces correct shapes for random input shapes
   - Reduction ops produce correct results for random input tensors (cross-check against naive impl)
   - Matmul associativity: (A*B)*C matches A*(B*C) within tolerance
3. Add proptest properties for ft-api:
   - Random tensor ops maintain shape consistency (output shape matches PyTorch rules)
   - save_state_dict -> load_state_dict round-trip preserves all values for random state dicts
4. Add adversarial fixture tests for ft-serialize:
   - Fuzzed JSON payloads don't cause panics (catch_unwind + verify Error returned)
   - Fuzzed binary payloads don't cause panics
   - Oversized shape dimensions don't cause OOM (our recent checked_mul fix)
5. Add adversarial tests for ft-nn:
   - NaN/Inf inputs to forward() produce NaN/Inf output (not panic)
   - Zero-sized batch inputs handled gracefully

Tests: The beads ARE the tests. Each property test should use proptest with 256+ cases.
Success criterion: cargo test --workspace --all-features passes with all new property tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:18:58.165045573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092380322Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:24:26.092126241Z","close_reason":"Added 8 property-based tests: 4 in ft-autograd (add/mul gradient correctness, backward determinism, relu gradient indicator) + 4 in ft-kernel-cpu (neg self-inverse, exp/log round-trip, sum vs naive, softmax sums-to-one). All pass with proptest 256+ cases. Added proptest dev-dependency to ft-kernel-cpu.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"frankentorch-0tw","title":"Add sparse tensor storage and basic sparse ops","description":"## Progress

### Completed
1. SparseCOOTensor in ft-core with indices [sparse_dim, nnz], values [nnz, *dense_dims], coalesced flag
2. SparseCSRTensor in ft-core with crow_indices, col_indices, values
3. SparseTensorError enum with comprehensive validation errors
4. AutogradError::SparseTensor variant for error propagation
5. ft-api sparse tensor methods
6. ft-kernel-cpu sparse operations
7. GradientValue enum (Dense/Sparse) in ft-autograd for unified gradient representation
8. sparse_gradients field in TensorBackwardReport for sparse gradient storage
9. Embedding.sparse field in ft-nn with with_options(sparse) constructor
10. SparseAdam now checks report for sparse gradients and iterates efficiently over COO entries
11. (this commit) Wired sparse backward path in IndexSelect when source tensor is marked sparse
12. (this commit) Added Embedding+SparseAdam roundtrip test
